LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF ERICA INGHAM, ON BEHALF OF HERSELF AND ALL OTHERS SIMILARLY SITATED'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED, IN PART. WITHIN 10 DAYS, PLAINTIFF MAY FILE AND SERVE HER SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE RESPONSE TO ADMISSION BY DEFENDANT CLUB ONE DEMONSTRATES THAT DEFENDANTS MAY HAVE TERMINATED PLAINTIFF IN RETALIATION FOR HER REQUEST FOR A PREGNANCY ACCOMMODATION. CALIFORNIA HAS A STRONG POLICY IN FAVOR OF GRANTING LEAVE TO AMEND THAT DICTATES PLAINTIFF SHOULD BE PERMITTED TO PLEAD HER NEW CAUSE OF ACTION. DISCOVERY IS REOPENED FOR THE LIMITED PURPOSE OF PERMITTING ALL PARTIES TO CONDUCT DISCOVERY AS TO THE NEW CAUSE OF ACTION. DEFENDANTS' REQUEST TO SHORTEN THE STATUTORY NOTICE TIME ON A MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED; THE COURT MAY NOT DO THIS WITHOUT ALL PARTIES' CONSENT. CCP 437C(A). PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT BE "DEEMED" FILED AND SERVED IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF MUST ACTUALLY FILE AND SERVE HER SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. (ORDER SIGNED AFTER HEARING.) MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File Second Amended Complt
Off Calendar Sep-16-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Edward Zappia, Esq. of the Zappia Law Firm, APC, Tel. 213.814.5550, for plaintiff and moving party by Court Call;
Francis D. Conway, Esq. of parton Sell Rhoades, Tel. 415.258.9700, for defendants and opposing party.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF ERICA INGHAM, ON BEHALF OF HERSELF AND ALL OTHERS SIMILARLY SITATED's MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File Second Amended Complaint is Granted, in part. Within 10 days, Plaintiff may file and serve her Second Amended Complaint. The response to admission by Defendant Club One demonstrates that Defendants may have terminated Plaintiff in retaliation for her request for a pregnancy accommodation. California has a strong policy in favor of granting leave to amend that dictates Plaintiff should be permitted to plead her new cause of action. Discovery is reopened for the limited purpose of permitting all parties to conduct discovery as to the new cause of action. Defendants' request to shorten the statutory notice time on a motion for summary adjudication is denied; the Court may not do this without all parties' consent. CCP 437c(a). Plaintiff's request for the Second Amended Complaint be "deemed" filed and served is denied. Plaintiff must actually file and serve her Second Amended Complaint. (Order signed after hearing.) Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185
